methods.description: Statistical procedures used to analyze the variability of pH, major ions, and nutrients in surveyed waters, and to evaluate diatom diversity and preferences.

steps.description: Variability of pH, major ions responsible for EC, and more abundant nutrients in the surveyed waters of different origin was compared with one-way ANOVA tests or ANOVA tests on ranks, after testing the normal distribution of the data through a Kolmogorov-Smirnov distance test.
steps.searchable_summary: perform ANOVA tests on water chemistry data after normality test

steps.description: Pairwise multiple comparison was performed following the Holm-Sidak method.
steps.searchable_summary: conduct pairwise multiple comparison using Holm-Sidak method

steps.description: Diatom α-diversity of epilithic and epiphytic assemblages was evaluated based on the Shannon Index calculated on number of identified individuals for each taxon.
steps.searchable_summary: calculate Shannon Index for diatom diversity

steps.description: The Evenness and Hill's index were computed to obtain information on the heterogeneity of the assemblages.
steps.searchable_summary: compute Evenness and Hill's index for diatom assemblages

steps.description: Preferences of the identified taxa for pH, total phosphorous concentrations, and moisture were evaluated according to the classification proposed by Van Dam et al.
steps.searchable_summary: evaluate diatom taxa preferences for environmental factors

steps.description: Non-metric multidimensional scaling (NMDS) was performed by R software to graphically evaluate differences between diatom assemblages.
steps.searchable_summary: perform NMDS analysis using R for diatom assemblages

steps.description: Stress values of NMDS configurations with increasing numbers of dimensions were compared to evaluate the stability of the analysis.
steps.searchable_summary: compare NMDS stress values for stability analysis

steps.description: A vector fitting analysis was applied to the sample scores on the final NMDS configuration using the R function envfit.
steps.searchable_summary: apply vector fitting analysis using envfit in R

steps.description: The relation between sample scores on the NMDS dimensions and the abundance of single diatom taxa was explored by correlation analysis.
steps.searchable_summary: perform correlation analysis between NMDS scores and diatom abundance

methods.description: Procedures for collecting water and diatom samples, measuring field parameters, and conducting laboratory analyses for chemical and diatom data.

steps.description: Water and diatom samples were collected in late summer 2016 under conditions of maximum melt water contribution.
steps.searchable_summary: collect water and diatom samples during peak melt water conditions

steps.description: Water temperature, electrical conductivity, and turbidity were measured in the field with portable meters.
steps.searchable_summary: measure water temperature, conductivity, and turbidity on site

steps.description: Water samples for chemical analyses were collected in acid washed HDPE bottles and analyzed for major ions and nutrients.
steps.searchable_summary: collect and analyze water samples for chemical composition

steps.description: Trace metal concentrations were determined by ICP-OES with ultrasonic nebulizer.
steps.searchable_summary: determine trace metal concentrations using ICP-OES

steps.description: Samples for diatom analysis were collected by brushing stones and preserved in ethanol.
steps.searchable_summary: collect and preserve diatom samples from stones

steps.description: Diatom samples were cleaned and mounted in Naphrax resin for microscopic analysis.
steps.searchable_summary: clean and mount diatom samples for microscopy

steps.description: At least 400 valves were identified and enumerated along transects of each permanent slide.
steps.searchable_summary: identify and count diatom valves on slides
